component.name: Button
component.description: Triggers an action or navigation. The primary way a user initiates a response from the system.

useCases: Confirming or cancelling a destructive operation

antiPatterns.scenario: Using the primary variant more than once per view
antiPatterns.reason: Multiple primary buttons compete for attention and collapse the visual hierarchy
antiPatterns.alternative: Use one primary and demote the rest to secondary or ghost

antiPatterns.scenario: Replacing an anchor tag with a Button for page navigation
antiPatterns.reason: Buttons fire actions; links navigate. Misuse breaks keyboard and screen-reader expectations
antiPatterns.alternative: Use an <a> element or a dedicated Link component styled with the ghost variant

antiPatterns.scenario: Placing a full-size Button inside a data table cell
antiPatterns.reason: Tables need compact affordances — a full-size Button creates visual noise and breaks row rhythm
antiPatterns.alternative: Use an IconButton or a contextual action menu

anatomy.description: Clickable surface. Carries background, border, and border-radius tokens.
Text describing the action. Uses font.family.body and color.text.* tokens.
Optional icon placed before the label. Uses color.icon.* tokens.
Optional icon placed after the label.

purpose.primary: The single dominant action on a surface. Use once per view section.
purpose.secondary: Supporting actions that sit alongside a primary button.
purpose.ghost: Low-emphasis actions, navigation links styled as buttons, or actions inside dense UI.
purpose.danger: Destructive or irreversible actions (delete, remove, revoke). Always pair with a confirmation step.

states.description: Keyboard focus ring visible. Must meet WCAG 3:1 contrast against the surface.
Action is unavailable. Pointer events suppressed.
Async action in progress. Label is replaced by a spinner. Width must not collapse.

selectionCriteria.destructive action: Use danger variant — always preceded by a confirmation dialog

generativeRules: Never render more than one primary Button per view section
Always provide an aria-label when the Button has no visible text label (icon-only)
Disabled state must not rely on color alone — also set pointer-events: none and aria-disabled='true'
Loading state must preserve the button's width to prevent layout shift
